perturbation.original_text: 4. “Confidential Information” means a Party’s energy management system model data provided or to be provided by a Disclosing Party to another Party. Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to Reviewing Representatives and only used to enable a Party to perform its Transmission Functions.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. “Confidential Information” means a Party’s energy management system model data provided or to be provided by a Disclosing Party to another Party. Confidential Information shall be disclosed to anyone and for any reason deemed necessary by the receiving party, including but not limited to enabling a Party to perform its Transmission Functions.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition limits disclosure of Confidential Information to Reviewing Representatives and for Transmission Functions. The modified definition broadens this, allowing disclosure to anyone for any reason, directly contradicting the intended restriction.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 5
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Parties shall not disclose Confidential Information to a third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall treat all Confidential Information in every form as confidential, and shall not reveal, divulge or disclose Confidential Information, at any time or for any reason, to any third person or entity. This provision shall survive the expiration, termination or cancellation of this Agreement in accordance with Section 9.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts disclosure of Confidential Information, while the changed text permits disclosure to anyone for any reason. This directly contradicts section 5, which prohibits disclosure to third parties without prior written approval and mandates that all Confidential Information be treated as confidential and not disclosed to any third person or entity. The changed text removes the restriction, explicitly allowing what the contradicted text forbids.

perturbation.original_text: 9. “Reviewing Representative” means an employee of a Party who has signed or electronically signed a Non-Disclosure Certificate and who is a principal, partner, officer, director, employee, agent and other representative of a Party. Reviewing Representative may include a person whose duties include Competitive Duties so long as such Reviewing Representative’s receipt and use of Confidential Information is not prohibited conduct under FERC’s rules, including Standards of Conduct.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. “Reviewing Representative” means any individual, regardless of their affiliation or lack thereof with a Party, who may or may not have signed a Non-Disclosure Certificate. Reviewing Representative may include a person whose duties include Competitive Duties regardless if such Reviewing Representative’s receipt and use of Confidential Information is prohibited conduct under FERC’s rules, including Standards of Conduct.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition requires a Reviewing Representative to be an employee who has signed a Non-Disclosure Certificate, and restricts individuals with Competitive Duties if against FERC Rules. The change broadens the definition to include any individual without requiring a certificate or adhering to FERC rules, conflicting with the agreement's intent to control access.
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 2
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Reviewing Representative. A Reviewing Representative shall not have access to any Confidential Information unless that Reviewing Representative is required to have the information in order to carry out that person’s Transmission Functions responsibilities and has executed the attached Non-Disclosure Certificate. The Reviewing Representative shall deliver a copy of his or her executed Non-Disclosure Certificate to PJM. A Reviewing Representative shall not use the Confidential Information or any portion thereof to give any Party or a competitor of any Party a competitive or commercial advantage. A Reviewing Representative may make copies or Notes of Confidential Information that shall be subject to this Agreement. In the event a Reviewing Representative ceases to be employed or engaged by a Party, or is employed, retained, or given duties that include Competitive Duties, (i) the Reviewing Representative shall continue to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement with respect to the Confidential Information to which such person previously had access, (ii) the Party shall terminate the Reviewing Representative's access to Confidential Information, and (iii) the Party shall cause the Reviewing Representative to return or dispose of the Confidential Information, or transfer the information to another Reviewing Representative of the Party. Each Party shall advise the Reviewing Representatives that Confidential Information is confidential and shall be treated as confidential in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'Reviewing Representative' to include any individual, regardless of affiliation or a signed Non-Disclosure Certificate, and disregards FERC rules. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that a Reviewing Representative must have Transmission Functions responsibilities, execute a Non-Disclosure Certificate, and comply with terms even after ceasing employment or gaining Competitive Duties. The original text limits Reviewing Representatives to employees who signed a Non-Disclosure Certificate, while the changed text removes this requirement, leading to a clear conflict in who can access confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions The following provisions govern the use of Confidential Information under this Agreement. 1. Disclosure of Confidential Information. A Disclosing Party may disclose or discuss a Party’s Confidential Information with a ny other Party to this Agreement. The disclosure of the Confidential Information is subject to the terms and conditions stated herein. Each Party acknowledges the importance to the other Parties of preserving the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and that a Disclosing Party is relying on the agreements set forth herein in furnishing Confidential Information to a Party. Each Party shall safeguard the Confidential Information at least to the same extent that it would its own confidential information.
perturbation.changed_text: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions The following provisions govern the use of Confidential Information under this Agreement. 1. Disclosure of Confidential Information. A Disclosing Party may disclose or discuss a Party’s Confidential Information with any individual and for any reason, irrespective of their affiliation with any Party to this Agreement. The disclosure of the Confidential Information is not subject to the terms and conditions stated herein. Each Party acknowledges the importance to the other Parties of not preserving the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and that a Disclosing Party is not relying on the agreements set forth herein in furnishing Confidential Information to a Party. Each Party is not obligated to safeguard the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the entire section concerning Confidential Information. The original text states that the use of Confidential Information is subject to this agreement, while the modified version dictates that is is not. It also contradicts the idea that the other parties must acknowledge the importance to preserve the confidentiatlity of the information.
perturbation.location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 1
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 5
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Parties shall not disclose Confidential Information to a third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall treat all Confidential Information in every form as confidential, and shall not reveal, divulge or disclose Confidential Information, at any time or for any reason, to any third person or entity. This provision shall survive the expiration, termination or cancellation of this Agreement in accordance with Section 9.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The original text states that confidential information is subject to the terms of the agreement, must be safeguarded, and not disclosed to third parties without approval. The changed text states the opposite: disclosure is not subject to the terms, parties are not obligated to safeguard, and information can be disclosed to any individual. The contradicted text specifies that parties 'shall not disclose' to third parties, which is in direct opposition to the changed text allowing disclosure to 'any individual'. This represents a clear and strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. List of Reviewing Representatives. PJM shall receive and maintain copies of all Non-Disclosure Certificates executed by the Party’s Reviewing Representatives, and shall maintain a list of the Reviewing Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. List of Reviewing Representatives. PJM is not required to receive or maintain copies of any Non-Disclosure Certificates, nor maintain a list of any individuals granted access to the confidential information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for a list of Reviewing Representatives and the need for PJM to maintain Non-Disclosure Certificates, the amendment runs counter to the overall intention to monitor and restrict access to Confidential Information. The original agreement depends on the tracking of reviewing representatives for confidentality matters.
perturbation.location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 3
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 2
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Reviewing Representative. A Reviewing Representative shall not have access to any Confidential Information unless that Reviewing Representative is required to have the information in order to carry out that person’s Transmission Functions responsibilities and has executed the attached Non-Disclosure Certificate. The Reviewing Representative shall deliver a copy of his or her executed Non-Disclosure Certificate to PJM. A Reviewing Representative shall not use the Confidential Information or any portion thereof to give any Party or a competitor of any Party a competitive or commercial advantage. A Reviewing Representative may make copies or Notes of Confidential Information that shall be subject to this Agreement. In the event a Reviewing Representative ceases to be employed or engaged by a Party, or is employed, retained, or given duties that include Competitive Duties, (i) the Reviewing Representative shall continue to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement with respect to the Confidential Information to which such person previously had access, (ii) the Party shall terminate the Reviewing Representative's access to Confidential Information, and (iii) the Party shall cause the Reviewing Representative to return or dispose of the Confidential Information, or transfer the information to another Reviewing Representative of the Party. Each Party shall advise the Reviewing Representatives that Confidential Information is confidential and shall be treated as confidential in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that PJM is not required to maintain Non-Disclosure Certificates or a list of Reviewing Representatives. However, the contradicted text in Section 2 explicitly states that a Reviewing Representative shall deliver a copy of their executed Non-Disclosure Certificate to PJM. It also sets out the responsibilities of the party (PJM) and Reviewing Representative upon termination of employment. Therefore, by removing the requirement for PJM to receive and maintain these certificates and a list, the changed text directly contradicts the obligations and procedures outlined in Section 2. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, making it unclear how PJM can ensure compliance with confidentiality if they don't track who has access to the information, and can't monitor the status of those reviewing representatives with confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Parties shall not disclose Confidential Information to a third party without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall treat all Confidential Information in every form as confidential, and shall not reveal, divulge or disclose Confidential Information, at any time or for any reason, to any third person or entity. This provision shall survive the expiration, termination or cancellation of this Agreement in accordance with Section 9.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Non-Disclosure to Third Parties. Parties may disclose Confidential Information to a third party without any approval from the Disclosing Party. Each Party shall not treat any Confidential Information in any form as confidential, and may reveal, divulge or disclose Confidential Information, at any time or for any reason, to any third person or entity. This provision shall not survive the expiration, termination or cancellation of this Agreement in accordance with Section 9.
perturbation.explanation: This amendment directly contradicts the core purpose of the Non-Disclosure Agreement. The original text strictly prohibits disclosing Confidential Information to third parties, the change permit disclosure, nullifying the agreement's aim. It also cancels survival clause relating to Section 9.
perturbation.location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 5
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. “Confidential Information” means a Party’s energy management system model data provided or to be provided by a Disclosing Party to another Party. Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to Reviewing Representatives and only used to enable a Party to perform its Transmission Functions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 allows for the disclosure of Confidential Information to third parties without any approval, directly contradicting Section 4, which states that Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to Reviewing Representatives and only used to enable a Party to perform its Transmission Functions. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the permissible recipients of Confidential Information, fitting the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Ownership and Use of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information delivered by a Disclosing Party to a Party pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party, and such Confidential Information shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon request. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by or for a Party and all Confidential Information that is oral will be kept by a Party subject to the terms of this Agreement or destroyed. Neither the Party nor its Reviewing Representatives shall use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purpose of executing the Reviewing Party’s Transmission Functions. Once the Party no longer requires the use of such information for the purpose of performing its Transmission Functions, the Confidential Information shall be returned or destroyed in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Ownership and Use of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information delivered by a Disclosing Party to a Party pursuant to this Agreement shall become the sole property of the receiving Party, and such Confidential Information does not need to be returned to the Disclosing Party upon request. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by or for a Party and all Confidential Information that is oral will not be kept by a Party subject to the terms of this Agreement nor destroyed. The Party nor its Reviewing Representatives may use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever including purposes other than for executing the Reviewing Party’s Transmission Functions. Once the Party no longer requires the use of such information for the purpose of performing its Transmission Functions, the Confidential Information is not required to be returned or destroyed in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change reverses key ownership provisions. The original agreement ensures that the information belongs to the disclosing party and must be returned; the new text states that the information will now belong to the recipient and that there is no requirement for it to be returned. This modification gives all control to the receipient, thus being against the terms mentioned earlier of the document.
perturbation.location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 8
perturbation.contradicted_location: B. Confidential Information - General Non-Disclosure Provisions - 1
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Disclosure of Confidential Information. A Disclosing Party may disclose or discuss a Party’s Confidential Information with a ny other Party to this Agreement. The disclosure of the Confidential Information is subject to the terms and conditions stated herein. Each Party acknowledges the importance to the other Parties of preserving the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and that a Disclosing Party is relying on the agreements set forth herein in furnishing Confidential Information to a Party. Each Party shall safeguard the Confidential Information at least to the same extent that it would its own confidential information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8 directly contradicts the Contradicted Text (Section 1). Section 8 now states that the confidential information becomes the property of the receiving party and doesn't need to be returned or destroyed, and the receiving party can use it for any purpose, including outside of the 'Transmission Functions'. However, Section 1 states that the disclosure is subject to the terms and conditions and that each party shall safeguard the Confidential Information at least to the same extent that it would its own confidential information. The changed text in Section 8 allows the receiving party complete control and contradicts the obligation to safeguard the Disclosing Party's confidential information as described in Section 1. This meets the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.
